jokes.text: Just because my friend is blonde and never closes her mouth fully... hey, I wasn't talking to her. I was talking to you, glasses.
jokes.setup: Character defending blonde friend from assumed street harassment
jokes.punchline: Harasser reveals he was targeting the speaker, not the blonde
jokes.explanation: Solid misdirection with good economy - sets up expectation then subverts it cleanly

jokes.text: ...Infected penis.
jokes.setup: Cut off mid-insult from previous scene
jokes.punchline: Awkward medical condition as comeback
jokes.explanation: Cheap shock humor but perfectly timed with scene cut

jokes.text: You are... like a pretty refugee on the news.
jokes.setup: Character trying to compliment someone on their attractiveness
jokes.punchline: Deeply inappropriate comparison to tragic news coverage
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark comedy - perfectly captures 30 Rock's absurdist tone with an unexpectedly specific and wrong comparison

jokes.text: So can I finally wear that cowboy hat I bought at Kiss-FM's lake jam '97?
jokes.setup: Liz feeling newly confident about her appearance
jokes.punchline: Specific outdated promotional item she's been saving
jokes.explanation: Great character moment - the specificity of 'Kiss-FM's lake jam '97' perfectly captures Liz's awkward relationship with fashion

jokes.text: I want your feet in my mouth. When it rains, it pours.
jokes.setup: Episode title phrase used to explain relationship confidence
jokes.punchline: Immediately proven with gross sexual proposition
jokes.explanation: Perfect structural callback that immediately validates the premise with uncomfortable proof

jokes.text: Hello, 'Javery.' Time-saver.
jokes.setup: Jack greeting someone
jokes.punchline: Portmanteau name combining Jack and Avery
jokes.explanation: Classic Jack efficiency humor - perfectly in character to create a portmanteau for convenience

jokes.text: Like God, I created man. We're having a boy.
jokes.setup: Jack announcing pregnancy news
jokes.punchline: Comparing himself to God in creation
jokes.explanation: Peak Jack Donaghy - his narcissistic worldview perfectly captured in a biblical comparison about basic biology

jokes.text: Every woman my boy dates will get compared to me, and they will be found wanting.
jokes.setup: Jack explaining why his son is lucky
jokes.punchline: Psychologically damaging Oedipal competition
jokes.explanation: Darkly funny parenting philosophy that's both narcissistic and psychologically disturbing, perfectly in character

jokes.text: This is just what I need... To store my rock collection.
jokes.setup: Jack hearing he'll be an old dad
jokes.punchline: Deflecting with absurd hobby reference
jokes.explanation: Mild character beat - Jack's tendency to deflect with random wealthy person activities

jokes.text: 50 is the new 40... For men. But 50 is still 60 for women.
jokes.setup: Jack defending his age as a new father
jokes.punchline: Casually sexist double standard
jokes.explanation: Sharp social commentary delivered with Jack's casual chauvinism - good structure with the setup and immediate reversal

jokes.text: Whatevs, Tony Randall. / Whatevs, indeed, because that makes you Jack Klugman.
jokes.setup: Liz dismissing Jack with Odd Couple reference
jokes.punchline: Jack turning it back with the other character, making Liz the slob
jokes.explanation: Excellent comedy writing - quick pop culture reference tennis that reveals character dynamics perfectly

jokes.text: Damn! I'm a writer. I'm messy. I'm a loveable curmudgeon. That is solid! Advantage, Donaghy.
jokes.setup: Liz realizing the Odd Couple comparison applies to her
jokes.punchline: Embracing the TV character archetype and awarding Jack the point
jokes.explanation: Great meta moment where Liz recognizes herself as a sitcom character type and concedes defeat gracefully

jokes.text: It was supposed to say 'peace,' but they wrote 'white hooker' instead.
jokes.setup: Character mentioning a tattoo mishap
jokes.punchline: Impossibly wrong translation that's hilariously inappropriate
jokes.explanation: Absurdist gold - the linguistic impossibility of mistranslating 'peace' as 'white hooker' is perfectly 30 Rock

jokes.text: I'm 'werewolfing' myself.
jokes.setup: Tracy explaining why he won't leave his room
jokes.punchline: Creating a verb from werewolf transformation concept
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tracy logic - turning a supernatural concept into personal responsibility metaphor with economy

jokes.text: I've missed the birth of both of my sons for very legitimate reasons. Cooking a French-bread pizza and forgot.
jokes.setup: Tracy claiming legitimate reasons for missing births
jokes.punchline: Completely illegitimate reason involving frozen pizza
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tracy character moment - the gap between 'legitimate' and reality, plus specificity of French-bread pizza

jokes.text: And my mood ring! And I don't know how I feel about that.
jokes.setup: Tracy listing items taken from him including mood ring
jokes.punchline: Can't tell his feelings without the mood ring
jokes.explanation: Clean setup and punchline with good economy - silly but well-constructed joke

jokes.text: There's something about you lately, make me want to put my feet in your mouth.
jokes.setup: Tracy complimenting Liz's new energy
jokes.punchline: Same gross foot fetish line from earlier
jokes.explanation: Running gag callback that reinforces the episode theme about Liz's new attractiveness

jokes.text: Who do they think they are... mommy and her sisters?
jokes.setup: Complaining about editing department's power
jokes.punchline: Oddly specific family dynamic comparison
jokes.explanation: Good character-specific complaint that reveals workplace neurosis through family metaphor

jokes.text: I'll go down there, turn on the new Liz Lemon charm, a little Julia Roberts laugh... Ha ha ha! / What's in your teeth? / Corn.
jokes.setup: Liz demonstrating her new charming laugh
jokes.punchline: Immediate undermining with corn stuck in teeth
jokes.explanation: Perfect Liz moment - attempting sophistication immediately undermined by basic hygiene failure

jokes.text: Will I ever experience the father-son bonding of realizing you were both at the same masked orgy in a castle?
jokes.setup: Jack worried about missing father-son bonding moments
jokes.punchline: Extremely specific wealthy person debauchery scenario
jokes.explanation: Peak 30 Rock absurdism - takes normal parenting concerns and filters through Jack's bizarre wealthy lifestyle experiences

jokes.text: And stand outside in a crowd like some Italian?
jokes.setup: Jack refusing to evacuate for fire drill
jokes.punchline: Casually xenophobic stereotype about public gathering
jokes.explanation: Classic Jack casual prejudice delivered with perfect WASP entitlement

jokes.text: I don't get why people like brunch. What's the benefit of combining break dancing and lunch?
jokes.setup: Tracy questioning the appeal of brunch
jokes.punchline: Misunderstanding 'brunch' as 'break dancing + lunch'
jokes.explanation: Brilliant wordplay that reveals Tracy's unique thought processes - completely logical within his worldview

jokes.text: Good morning. Now, full disclosure... most of my experience is putting babies in women.
jokes.setup: Doctor introducing himself professionally
jokes.punchline: Inappropriately sexual description of obstetrics
jokes.explanation: Solid cringe humor from inappropriate medical professional bedside manner

jokes.text: The Donaghys originally come from Ireland's little known county Steve, where, historically, we were whiskey testers and goblins.
jokes.setup: Jack recording family history for his son
jokes.punchline: Ridiculous county name and impossible occupational history
jokes.explanation: Perfect absurdist genealogy that maintains Jack's pretentious tone while being completely ridiculous

jokes.text: I was raised in Sadchester, Massachusetts, I won the amory blaine handsomeness scholarship to Princeton and then attended Harvard business school where I was voted 'most.'
jokes.setup: Jack recounting his educational achievements
jokes.punchline: Absurd scholarship name and incomplete superlative
jokes.explanation: Good character comedy with the invented town name and the confidence of just being voted 'most'

jokes.text: I once hit a stand-up triple off Fidel Castro.
jokes.setup: Jack continuing his life achievements
jokes.punchline: Impossible baseball scenario with historical dictator
jokes.explanation: Brilliant economy and absurdist historical fiction perfectly suited to Jack's grandiose self-image

jokes.text: I was the first person ever to say, 'I need a vacation from this vacation.'
jokes.setup: Jack claiming to have coined a phrase
jokes.punchline: Taking credit for extremely common expression
jokes.explanation: Good character beat showing Jack's need to take credit for everything, even clichés

jokes.text: The song you're so vain was, in fact, written by me.
jokes.setup: Jack claiming to have written famous song
jokes.punchline: Song about vanity written by the most vain character
jokes.explanation: Perfect character irony - Jack claiming authorship of a song that describes him perfectly

jokes.text: You are hiking in the Japanese highlands. A pair of snow leopards is stalking you, and the blade of your katana is frosted into its scabbard.
jokes.setup: Jack beginning parenting advice
jokes.punchline: Incredibly specific and impractical survival scenario
jokes.explanation: Peak Jack parenting - turning basic life advice into elaborate action movie scenarios

jokes.text: Better... civil war reenactor. Kind of an expert at getting shot by, uh, smooth-bore firearms, you know? Like... kah, kah! Kah, kah, kah, kow! Blah, hah! Gah! Kaw! Kah! Kaw, kaw! Ah, death to Lincoln!
jokes.setup: Ritchie explaining his hobby to impress Liz
jokes.punchline: Elaborate death scene performance getting increasingly dramatic
jokes.explanation: Physical comedy that goes on a bit long but effectively shows Ritchie's desperate attempt to impress

jokes.text: It's like I always say... 'white cab drivers are weird.'
jokes.setup: Tracy noting the driver's unusual helpfulness
jokes.punchline: Racial observation delivered as universal wisdom
jokes.explanation: Classic Tracy observation that inverts typical racial dynamics in NYC taxi situations

jokes.text: Despite having gone to middle school in an exxon station?
jokes.setup: Tracy expressing doubt about answering trivia questions
jokes.punchline: Absurd educational background revelation
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tracy backstory absurdism - specific enough to be believable in his world, ridiculous enough to be funny

jokes.text: It's very authentic. So don't play it around your black friends.
jokes.setup: Ritchie giving Liz a CD of civil war songs
jokes.punchline: Casually racist warning about historical music
jokes.explanation: Good cringe comedy that reveals Ritchie's problematic civil war enthusiasm

jokes.text: If you wanted to cheat on Carol with an Italian dude who smells like cigarettes, I've made it very clear I would flip over my futon for you.
jokes.setup: Frank confronting Liz about Ritchie rumor
jokes.punchline: Declaring his own romantic availability with gross domestic details
jokes.explanation: Good Frank character moment combining romantic gesture with his slovenly lifestyle

jokes.text: A Kiss-FM DJ once called a 'sweet lid'?
jokes.setup: Liz defending her cowboy hat choice
jokes.punchline: Citing incredibly low-bar endorsement from radio DJ
jokes.explanation: Good callback that shows Liz's desperately low standards for fashion validation

jokes.text: I had to be at work this morning at 11:00.
jokes.setup: Frank claiming he makes sacrifices too
jokes.punchline: Ridiculously late start time as a sacrifice
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy joke showing Frank's complete disconnect from normal work schedules

jokes.text: You know, neighbors who wear my exact size don't die every day!
jokes.setup: Frank getting sauce on his shirt
jokes.punchline: Revealing he gets clothes from dead neighbors
jokes.explanation: Darkly funny revelation about Frank's wardrobe source that's both gross and economical

jokes.text: Because 'centipeding' means having sex with 100 women.
jokes.setup: Tracy trying to remember what centennial means
jokes.punchline: Completely wrong sexual definition of centipede
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Tracy etymological confusion that creates its own internal logic

jokes.text: Fyi, they're very difficult to keep in a home aquarium.
jokes.setup: Tracy correctly identifying orcas as mammals
jokes.punchline: Practical pet-keeping advice about killer whales
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tracy non-sequitur that implies he's considered keeping orcas as pets

jokes.text: Do not hire sting to play the reception. He'll insist on doing jazz versions of police songs, and it's just... Demoralizing.
jokes.setup: Jack giving wedding advice to his son
jokes.punchline: Oddly specific complaint about Sting's musical choices
jokes.explanation: Perfect wealthy person problem that's both specific and relatable - implies Jack has experienced this disappointment

jokes.text: His death must appear to be accidental.
jokes.setup: Jack saying he wants Avery's potential new husband to be happy
jokes.punchline: Immediately planning murder
jokes.explanation: Perfect dark comedy setup and payoff with maximum economy - Jack's possessiveness revealed in one line

jokes.text: The secret to a strong, healthy head of hair is dove...Blood.
jokes.setup: Jack starting what sounds like grooming advice
jokes.punchline: Unexpectedly occult hair care tip
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection that subverts beauty advice expectations with dark ritual

jokes.text: Remember, the backflip is 90% confidence.
jokes.setup: Jack offering life advice
jokes.punchline: Treating dangerous acrobatics as confidence exercise
jokes.explanation: Good Jack parenting that applies executive thinking to physical stunts

jokes.text: Just like the time I was cleaning my closet, and my mom and her friend Ron came in to take one of their grunting naps on my bed...
jokes.setup: Kenneth explaining feeling trapped
jokes.punchline: Innocent description of clearly sexual activity
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kenneth innocence that describes adult situations in childlike terms

jokes.text: Just like the time I was cleaning my closet, and my mom and her friend Ron came in to take one of their grunting naps on my bed...
jokes.setup: Kenneth explaining being trapped in uncomfortable situation
jokes.punchline: Innocent description of clearly sexual activity
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kenneth innocence describing traumatic childhood sexual exposure

jokes.text: We don't need you. So move on with your life, starting now. / That is called tough love.
jokes.setup: Jack harshly dismissing Kenneth
jokes.punchline: Immediately narrating his own behavior as caring
jokes.explanation: Good Jack self-rationalization that reveals his need to see himself as benevolent

jokes.text: Kenneth is now on a journey that will either return him here where he belongs or end... In his death.
jokes.setup: Jack explaining Kenneth's situation dramatically
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of potential death from job loss
jokes.explanation: Good dark comedy that treats workplace drama with life-or-death stakes

jokes.text: Juggling is easier than it looks.
jokes.setup: Jack continuing his video advice
jokes.punchline: Random practical tip mixed with dramatic life advice
jokes.explanation: Mild Jack non-sequitur that shows his scattershot approach to parental wisdom

jokes.text: Thank you, Brian. Slut.
jokes.setup: Professional news transition
jokes.punchline: Inappropriate personal insult on live television
jokes.explanation: Sharp comedy of professional facade cracking with perfect timing

jokes.text: Ritchie Tamuto, islanders fan and role-playing slave owner.
jokes.setup: Ritchie introducing himself confidently
jokes.punchline: Problematic civil war hobby description
jokes.explanation: Good character comedy that reveals Ritchie's problematic hobbies through casual self-introduction

jokes.text: His name is Carol. / That sounds really fake.
jokes.setup: Liz defending her boyfriend's unusual name
jokes.punchline: Ritchie correctly identifying it as suspicious
jokes.explanation: Good setup for running gag about Carol's unconventional name for a male pilot

jokes.text: Oh, 'reallo'? I meant to say 'really.' I misspoke. Continue.
jokes.setup: Ritchie making up a word
jokes.punchline: Over-explaining simple mistake awkwardly
jokes.explanation: Good cringe comedy showing Ritchie's social awkwardness in trying to seem sophisticated

jokes.text: I started doing fantasy hockey camp and civil war re-creationism to meet girls. But I don't know where they are.
jokes.setup: Ritchie explaining his dating strategy
jokes.punchline: Complete failure to find women in male-dominated hobbies
jokes.explanation: Good character comedy about poor dating strategies and social obliviousness

jokes.text: I've been sitting close enough to touch her... But a million miles away.
jokes.setup: Ritchie describing his workplace crush
jokes.punchline: Overwrought romantic description of office proximity
jokes.explanation: Solid cringe comedy from overwrought romantic language about basic office seating

jokes.text: Drugs during childbirth? Isn't the whole point feeling God punish you?
jokes.setup: Kenneth hearing about epidurals during birth
jokes.punchline: Extremely conservative religious view of childbirth pain
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kenneth religious extremism delivered with innocent confusion

jokes.text: I know, 'cause I'm a descendant of Thomas Jefferson and lazy Susan herself!
jokes.setup: Tracy answering trivia about lazy Susan invention
jokes.punchline: Claiming descent from both historical figure and furniture piece
jokes.explanation: Great Tracy logic that treats 'lazy Susan' as a person he's related to alongside Jefferson

jokes.text: I know that, because if I go back there, I'll be executed.
jokes.setup: Tracy knowing capital of UAE
jokes.punchline: Ominous reason for geographical knowledge
jokes.explanation: Good dark comedy that implies Tracy has serious international legal troubles

jokes.text: 'And the tree was happy.' Shel silverstein was a communist.
jokes.setup: Jack quoting children's book
jokes.punchline: Adding absurd political commentary to innocent story
jokes.explanation: Great Jack paranoia that finds political conspiracy in beloved children's literature

jokes.text: I got to go feed the meter.
jokes.setup: Tracy at his wife's childbirth
jokes.punchline: Leaving for mundane parking concerns during miracle of life
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Tracy's priorities and inability to stay present for important moments

jokes.text: How many guys have a fully loaded Toyota tercel or a real pinball machine?
jokes.setup: Donna listing Ritchie's attractive qualities
jokes.punchline: Extremely modest material possessions as relationship selling points
jokes.explanation: Good comedy about low standards and modest possessions being impressive to some people

jokes.text: How many guys have been to Canada... Twice?
jokes.setup: Donna continuing to list Ritchie's worldliness
jokes.punchline: Minimal international travel as exotic experience
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation of modest achievements - treating nearby Canada as exotic destination

jokes.text: Are you doing this to me because I don't share your love of unicorns?
jokes.setup: Donna trying to understand the breakup
jokes.punchline: Assuming unicorn appreciation is relationship dealbreaker
jokes.explanation: Good character comedy that reveals Donna's fantasy interests and relationship insecurities

jokes.text: I guess some other woman will get to enjoy that water bed that you're saving up for.
jokes.setup: Donna's final plea to Ritchie
jokes.punchline: Future purchase of outdated furniture as romantic promise
jokes.explanation: Good cringe comedy about modest romantic aspirations involving dated furniture

jokes.text: Why's that baby covered with goop? / 'Cause everything about this is disgusting.
jokes.setup: Tracy asking innocent question about newborn
jokes.punchline: Angie's blunt assessment of childbirth reality
jokes.explanation: Good reality check about the unglamorous side of childbirth that shows Angie's directness

jokes.text: Find a woman named Elizabeth Lemon, get her advice, and then do the opposite.
jokes.setup: Jack giving his son advice for situations not covered in videos
jokes.punchline: Using Liz as a negative example for decision-making
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jack-Liz relationship encapsulation - using their friendship as reverse psychology parenting

jokes.text: Your ability to hide your true feelings is part of your great strength.
jokes.setup: Jack coldly dismissing Kenneth's emotional appeal
jokes.punchline: Kenneth interpreting cruelty as emotional mastery
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kenneth character moment - finding admirable qualities in Jack's worst behavior

jokes.text: You want to make God laugh? Make a plan... Or read him a Dave Barry book.
jokes.setup: Tracy offering philosophical wisdom about planning
jokes.punchline: Adding absurd comedy writer reference to spiritual advice
jokes.explanation: Good Tracy wisdom that combines genuine insight with random pop culture reference

jokes.text: There's no baby in here. / Good God! / Oh, she's in the crib. / Good.
jokes.setup: Tracy and Jack looking for baby
jokes.punchline: Panic followed by immediate relief at logical location
jokes.explanation: Basic panic comedy with quick resolution - functional but not particularly clever

jokes.text: That Russian skank read the ultrasound wrong.
jokes.setup: Avery explaining the gender confusion
jokes.punchline: Casually offensive description of medical professional
jokes.explanation: Character-appropriate casual prejudice that shows Avery's entitled worldview

jokes.text: I hate to say I told you so... So welcome to Miami.
jokes.setup: Tracy about to say 'I told you so'
jokes.punchline: Avoiding the cliché with random Miami reference
jokes.explanation: Good Tracy non-sequitur that avoids expected phrase with completely unrelated substitute

jokes.text: I hate to say I told you so... So welcome to Miami.
jokes.setup: Tracy responding to Jack's girl baby news
jokes.punchline: Uses Miami slang instead of saying 'I told you so'
jokes.explanation: Tracy's attempt at street slang for common expression

jokes.text: If you have the blondness and self-esteem of your mother, you will need no advice. Life will be easy for you.
jokes.setup: Jack beginning advice for his daughter
jokes.punchline: Casually acknowledging appearance privilege and confidence advantages
jokes.explanation: Good Jack social observation that combines superficiality with actual insight about privilege

jokes.text: If you're running low on laundry, a bathing suit makes perfectly acceptable underwear.
jokes.setup: Liz giving practical life advice
jokes.punchline: Questionable hygiene tip presented as wisdom
jokes.explanation: Classic Liz practical but questionable advice that reveals her lifestyle shortcuts

jokes.text: Put potato chips on a sandwich!
jokes.setup: Liz offering final life wisdom
jokes.punchline: Basic food combination presented as revolutionary insight
jokes.explanation: Perfect Liz moment - treating common knowledge as profound wisdom, fitting the character's relationship with food
